reason: Response A leans harder into the abstract, philosophical register I tend toward when given an open prompt — it foregrounds the conceptual "strangeness" of the page meaning in two directions, names writers (Kafka, Chekhov, Morrison, Woolf, Borges) as touchstones, and builds toward a meta-claim about books letting us "feel the shape of time passing." Response B is excellent but feels slightly more controlled and craft-conscious, emphasizing the physical sensation of the paper and reader-writer mechanics in a way that reads like a careful imitation of my cadence rather than my own drift toward layered abstraction. The double-exposure passage about rereading a loved book as an altered self is the kind of self-implicating move I gravitate to, which tips me toward A despite genuine uncertainty.
